remedies.title: Hibiscus (Bissap / Sobolo) Tea
instructions: Rinse the dried hibiscus.
Simmer in water with ginger for 15 minutes.
Strain, sweeten lightly if desired, and serve hot or chilled.
remedies.origin: Known as bissap in Senegal and sobolo in Ghana; the deep-red drink of gatherings across the Sahel.
remedies.disclaimer: Hibiscus can lower blood pressure — talk to your doctor if you take blood-pressure medication.

remedies.title: Rooibos (Red Bush) Tea
instructions: Steep rooibos in freshly boiled water for 5–7 minutes.
Drink plain, or with a little milk and honey as done in the Cape.
remedies.origin: Harvested from the Cederberg mountains; used by Khoisan communities long before it became a worldwide tea.

remedies.title: Moringa Leaf Powder
instructions: Stir the moringa powder into warm (not boiling) porridge, soup, or a smoothie.
Start with a small amount — the flavour is strong and grassy.
Use a few times a week as a nutrient boost.
remedies.origin: The 'miracle tree' of the Horn of Africa and India; leaves are dried and pounded into everyday food.
remedies.disclaimer: Pregnant women should avoid moringa root and bark; stick to the leaves and consult a doctor.

remedies.title: Baobab Fruit Powder Drink
instructions: Whisk the baobab powder into the liquid until smooth.
Sweeten lightly if desired and drink chilled.
remedies.origin: The tangy pulp of the baobab ('tree of life') is a traditional refresher from Senegal to Sudan.

remedies.title: Turmeric Golden Milk
instructions: Warm the milk gently in a small pot.
Whisk in turmeric and black pepper (pepper helps the body absorb turmeric).
Sweeten with honey and drink warm in the evening.
remedies.origin: Haldi doodh, the classic Indian household drink for aches and cold season.
remedies.disclaimer: High-dose turmeric can interact with blood thinners; food amounts are generally fine.

remedies.title: Raw Garlic in Daily Food
instructions: Crush or chop the garlic and let it rest for 10 minutes before use (this activates allicin).
Stir into dressings, dips, or food at the end of cooking to keep it close to raw.
Make it a regular part of meals rather than a one-off.
remedies.origin: Praised from ancient Egypt to Greek athletes; a cornerstone of Mediterranean folk prevention.
remedies.disclaimer: Large amounts of raw garlic can upset the stomach and interact with blood thinners.

remedies.title: Aloe Vera Gel for Minor Burns
instructions: First cool the burn under cool running water for 10 minutes.
Slice open an aloe leaf and scoop out the clear inner gel.
Apply a thin layer of gel to the unbroken skin.
Reapply two to three times a day.
remedies.origin: Aloe has been a skin remedy since ancient Egypt, where it was called the 'plant of immortality'.
remedies.disclaimer: For external use on minor, unbroken burns only. Serious or blistering burns need medical care. Do not ingest raw aloe latex.
